desc: Tirana is a fast-changing Balkan capital where Ottoman-era landmarks, communist history, and youthful café districts coexist in a compact, affordable city break. It works well year-round, with strongest comfort in spring and autumn and lively summer evenings before mountain/coast side trips.

todo.name: Bunk'Art 1 & 2
todo.desc: Cold War bunkers transformed into arresting museums — utterly unlike anything else in Europe.

todo.name: Skanderbeg Square
todo.desc: Grand central plaza framed by the National History Museum's mosaic façade and the Et'hem Bey Mosque.

todo.name: Mount Dajti cable car
todo.desc: A 15-min gondola to 1,613 m with sweeping panoramas over the city and mountains.

todo.name: Krujë Castle day trip
todo.desc: Hilltop Ottoman fortress and traditional bazaar — birthplace of Skanderbeg. ~40 min from the city.

todo.name: Blloku district
todo.desc: Once exclusive to communist elites — now Tirana's most vibrant area of cafes and galleries.

todo.name: Apollonia archaeological park
todo.desc: Ancient Greek colony ruins on a bucolic hillside, largely undiscovered by mass tourism.

todo.name: Durrës
todo.desc: 40-min drive to the Adriatic and one of the largest Roman amphitheatres in the Balkans.

todo.name: Albanian Alps (Valbona / Theth)
todo.desc: World-class hiking in the dramatic Accursed Mountains. Best as a 2–3 day extension.

wifi.note: Generally reliable across central districts and business cafés; local eSIM data is a useful backup for regional day trips.

prac.fltNote: Direct flights are available from Gatwick; London City typically requires a connection. Return fares are often ~£40–£110 off-peak, ~£110–£190 in shoulder demand, and ~£190+ around summer and major holiday peaks.
prac.visa: Visa-free for UK citizens for short stays (typically up to 90 days in a 180-day period).
alerts: Summer traffic can slow city-to-coast transfers, especially weekend departures.
Card acceptance is improving but still uneven outside central zones — keep some local cash.
Heat and sun exposure can be significant on hilltop day trips in midsummer.

sweet: April–May and October are usually the best-value windows for warm weather and manageable city crowds.
